title: From afar, American students in France feel the shockwaves of Trump
subhead: As academic freedom declines in the US, some young people are choosing France to pursue master's and doctoral degrees. Yet, between delayed scholarships, intrusive questions and stereotypes, their experience remains shaken by the latest developments in their country.

In southern Lebanon, civilians are trapped after Israel destroys bridges
subhead: The Israeli military is seeking to isolate southern Lebanon, a Hezbollah stronghold, from the rest of the country. Now, only one access route connects Tyre's remaining 20,000 residents and displaced people to the capital.

title: Italy: Giorgia Meloni's failed gamble on judicial reform
subhead: By overwhelmingly voting against the referendum pushed by Italian PM Giorgia Meloni, a majority of Italians have expressed their attachment to a certain vision of democracy and to the constitution forged after Mussolini's dictatorship.

title: Five affordable vegetarian restaurants in the Paris region
subhead: Craving a quinoa arancini, an Asian-inspired salad or a coconut dessert? Here is our selection of places in Paris and the suburbs where you can enjoy quality vegetarian food for under €20.

France's traditional parties dream of a return to the classic left-right contest
subhead: The Socialists and right-wing Les Républicains can boast of successes in the second round of France's municipal elections on March 22. But both parties remain deeply weakened by the lack of a clear direction and the fragmentation of the political landscape, writes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

title: TotalEnergies bows to Trump administration, dropping two offshore wind projects
subhead: The French company is ending two offshore wind turbine programs off the coasts of New York and North Carolina, totaling 4 gigawatts of power. It will receive a $928 million reimbursement on the condition that the funds are reinvested in hydrocarbon projects.
